response.justification: The candidate has strong general software engineering fundamentals and communication skills, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. C++ experience (6 months at National Instruments in 2007–2008) is 15+ years old and represents only a small portion of a career trajectory that has moved entirely toward web/full-stack development with modern languages. Mandatory requirements for 5–10 years of current C++ development on Windows, SQL Server/Oracle expertise, and capital markets or investment banking domain experience are all critically absent from the resume.
strengths: Demonstrates strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pair programming) and testing discipline throughout career
Extensive full-stack experience across multiple languages and platforms with proven ability to ship production systems
Excellent communication and mentoring track record, evidenced by workshops, talks, and open-source project leadership
concerns: C++ experience is 15+ years old (2007–2008, only 6 months at National Instruments); career has since moved entirely to TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web technologies
No demonstrated expertise with SQL Server, Oracle, or Windows platform development; resume lists generic SQL alongside MongoDB and MySQL
Zero domain experience in capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking; career focused on web, GIS, fintech infrastructure, and tooling rather than financial services

key_factors.factor: Capital markets or fixed income domain knowledge
SQL Server/Oracle and Windows platform expertise
